input.background: Scores derived from a patient satisfaction survey are Normally distributed with μ=50 and σ=7.5, with high scores indicating high satisfaction. An SRS of n=36 is taken from this population.
input.question: Suppose a sample of n=36 finds an x̄ of 48.8. Mark this finding on the horizontal axis of your sketch. Then calculate the z_stat for the result. Does this observation provide strong evidence against H_0?

output.answer: Notice that z_stat=(48.8-50)/1.25=-0.96. Therefore, 48.8 is a little less than 1 standard deviation below μ_0. This would not be unusual and would not provide strong evidence against H_0.

mu.description: Population mean of the patient satisfaction scores.

sigma.description: Population standard deviation of the patient satisfaction scores.

n.description: Sample size taken from the population.

x_bar.description: Sample mean of the patient satisfaction scores.

z_stat.description: Z-statistic calculated from the sample mean and population parameters.
